title: Ensure Server Parameter 'log_checkpoints' is set to 'ON' for  PostgreSQL Database Server

input_row.title: Ensure Server Parameter 'log_checkpoints' is set to 'ON' for  PostgreSQL Database Server
input_row.rationale: Enabling log_checkpoints helps the PostgreSQL Database to Log each checkpoint in turn generates query and error logs. However, access to transaction logs is not supported. Query and error logs can be used to identify, troubleshoot, and repair configuration errors and sub-optimal performance.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Go to Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers. 3. For each database, click on Server parameters. 4. Search for log_checkpoints. 5. Ensure that value is set to ON. From Azure CLI Ensure value is set to ON az postgres server configuration show --resource-group - -server-name --name log_checkpoints From PowerShell Ensure value is set to ON Get-AzPostgreSqlConfiguration -ResourceGroupName - ServerName -Name log_checkpoints From Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: eb6f77b9-bd53-4e35-a23d-7f65d5f0e43d - Name: 'Log checkpoints should be enabled for PostgreSQL database servers'
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Go to Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers. 3. For each database, click on Server parameters. 4. Search for log_checkpoints. 5. Click ON and save. From Azure CLI Use the below command to update log_checkpoints configuration. az postgres server configuration set --resource-group -- server-name --name log_checkpoints --value on From PowerShell Update-AzPostgreSqlConfiguration -ResourceGroupName - ServerName -Name log_checkpoints -Value on
input_row.default_value: By default log_checkpoints is enabled (set to on).

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be verified through AWS APIs and SDKs, specifically for RDS instances that may utilize PostgreSQL as the database engine. 
- Audit contains specific commands for verifying the configuration.
- It aligns with standard AWS features including logging and configuration settings, which can be assessed programmatically.
- Since the control is related to configurations of the database server, it falls within the domain of AWS managed services which can be validated using APIs, thus it does not require manual inspection or SSH access to compute instances.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_rds_postgresql_db_instance_log_checkpoints_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI or boto3 SDK to enumerate RDS instances and check the configuration settings.
- AWS CLI command: `aws rds describe-db-instances --query "DBInstances[*].[DBInstanceIdentifier,DBParameterGroups[*].DBParameterGroupName]"` to list all RDS DB instances and their parameter groups.
- Use the boto3 SDK with the method `client.describe_db_parameters(DBParameterGroupName)`.
- Validate if the parameter `log_checkpoints` is set to `ON`.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: If the parameter `log_checkpoints` exists and its Value is `ON`.
  - Fail: If the parameter either does not exist, or its Value is not `ON`.
- This check can be performed across all PostgreSQL RDS instances by ensuring the appropriate parameter group is associated with them.
